sections.heading: This Directive harmonises the provisions of the Member
sections.title: Subscriber directory information disclosure
sections.summary: Member States must ensure subscribers are informed free of charge before inclusion in public directories about directory purposes, data usage, and search functions. This applies to printed or electronic directories available to the public or through directory enquiry services.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires transparency in directory data usage, impacting how financial institutions share customer data across borders through public directories.

requirements.text: Member States shall ensure subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services

Member States shall ensure subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory

sections.source_quote: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services, in which their personal data can be included and of any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory.
sections.relevance_reason: Directly addresses data subject rights and disclosure requirements

sections.heading: This Directive shall not apply to activities which fall
sections.title: Working Party on individual data protection tasks
sections.summary: The Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data (established under Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC) must carry out tasks related to protecting fundamental rights and freedoms and legitimate interests in the electronic communications sector.
sections.dtia_summary: Does not directly impact data transfer impact assessments as it is a procedural task for a specific working group, not a data transfer requirement.
sections.source_quote: The Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data instituted by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC shall also carry out the tasks laid down in Article 30 of that Directive with regard to matters covered by this Directive, namely the protection of fundamental rights and freedoms and of legitimate interests in the electronic communications sector.

sections.heading: In case of a particular risk of a breach of the security of
sections.title: Member States' obligation to report national law to Commission
sections.summary: Member States must report to the European Commission the text of national laws they adopt implementing this Directive and any subsequent amendments. The Commission is required to submit a report to the European Parliament and Council within three years of the Directive's entry into force, assessing its application and economic impact, particularly regarding unsolicited communications. The Commission may request information from Member States without undue delay to inform this report.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires Member States to report national laws affecting data transfers, relevant for understanding jurisdictional boundaries in cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: Member States shall communicate to the Commission the text of national law provisions adopted in the field governed by this Directive
Member States shall communicate any subsequent amendments to national law provisions
Commission shall submit report to European Parliament and Council within three years of Directive entry into force
Commission may request information from Member States without undue delay

sections.source_quote: Member States shall communicate to the Commission the text of the provisions of national law which they adopt in the field governed by this Directive and of any subsequent amendments to those provisions.

sections.heading: For the purpose of marketing electronic communications

sections.heading: The service provider must inform the subscriber or user
sections.title: Informing subscribers about traffic data processing
sections.summary: Service providers must inform subscribers about the types of traffic data processed and the duration of processing for billing and interconnection purposes. This disclosure must occur prior to obtaining consent for marketing activities.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates transparency about traffic data processing duration and scope, critical for DTIA compliance in telecom data transfers.

requirements.text: Service provider must inform subscriber of types of traffic data processed
Service provider must inform subscriber of duration of processing for billing and interconnection purposes
Service provider must inform subscriber of purposes before obtaining consent for marketing

sections.source_quote: 4. The service provider must inform the subscriber or user of the types of traffic data which are processed and of the duration of such processing for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 2 and, prior to obtaining consent, for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 3.
sections.relevance_reason: Directly addresses consent and data disclosure for cross-border context

sections.heading: Processing of traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs
sections.title: Restrictions on traffic data processing recipients
sections.summary: Traffic data processing must be limited to persons authorized by providers of public communications networks and services handling billing, traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing, or value-added services. Processing must be restricted to what is necessary for these activities.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines permissible recipients for traffic data transfers, ensuring only authorized entities handle data for billing and service purposes across borders.

requirements.text: Processing must be restricted to persons acting under authority of providers of public communications networks
Processing must be restricted to persons handling billing or traffic management
Processing must be restricted to persons handling customer enquiries
Processing must be restricted to persons handling fraud detection
Processing must be restricted to persons handling marketing electronic communications services
Processing must be restricted to persons providing value added services
Processing must be restricted to what is necessary for such activities

sections.source_quote: 5. Processing of traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4, must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of the public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services handling billing or traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing electronic communications services or providing a value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.
